reviews.review_msg: Dr. Timcke is often regarded as a misunderstood lecturer however, do what is asked such as reading and making use of office hours and you will be fine.

Dr. Scott Timcke is a GREAT lecturer. I am a student in is Communication Lecture and he is amazing! He gives great feedback. A lot of the students think that his lectures are confusing and he is a tough grader but this is simply not true. If you don't attend his lectures, how do you expect to understand what he is teaching? Excellent Lecturer!
Confusing lectures where I feel lost after every class Makes us feel that the standard he set is too high as half of the class failed the mid term

Professor Scott Timecke is smart and sophisticated in his course. He is too smart, which makes the students to have a bit distance from him. Also, he is a bit rude in his way of communication. Perhaps, some humility is needed to achieve full potentials.

At first it may seem like you're doomed for the rest of the semester but Scott isn't really all that bad. He always has good inputs on your assignments, but can also give you the honest, brutal truth of what else is lacking in your work.

Literally the worst teacher I have ever had in my 4 years at SFU.  I had to 'unlearn' how to write a good paper. a paper with a thesis is a bad thing for him.  I hope you like negative feedback, because every time he hands back an assignment he spends half an hour of class time tell everyone how terrible they ALL were! Good Riddance.

Scott's course was a challenge, no doubt, but it was equally rewarding. Scott clearly really cares about his students' learning (he stated this explicitly multiple times), and to that end he let us write multiple drafts of our papers before receiving a final grade on them - I wish every class allowed this.

I learned from Scott while he was a TA, during then there was quite the buzz of him being difficult. unluckily (or luckily) I didn't hear about this until the semester started. He's difficult but he's helpful if you visit his office hours and actually do what you're supposed to do. READ! He's clear on what he wants and is actually very reasonable .

He expects you to read A LOT (not specifically courseware), and looks down at you when you don't read. He is rude to his students, says our writing is sh!t because we don't read but doesn't explain himself. Lectures long and boring, and talks sh!t about other professors. Only took it cause it was 6 credits

Scott was definitely very intimidating at first. His lectures are hard to follow, mainly because of his accent and language. But once you talk to him in person, you start to understand exactly what he's looking for, and how you can excel in his class. Highly recommend for people who are looking to LEARN.

He expects you to be enthusiastic about his class, but he hates teaching so it makes it difficult to want to try.  His lectures do not correspond to his readings. His accent is very difficult to understand. He knows the material well, but often reads from his notes like he's reading from an essay (boring lectures). Marking is fair.

Scott's lectures could be interesting at times but his vocabulary is way beyond his students (espicially for a first-level class). He tended to lecture on things beyond the scope of the course in order to truly make it a full overview of cmns. 3 Papers at 30% each and 10% for participation makes for an easy class if you can write.

Incredibly knowledgeable and lectures are worth it even though they can be difficult to follow sometimes. Expectations are high, but that isn't a bad thing. Make sure to read and be ready to participate including leading discussion in order to receive ANY participation marks. Go to Scott and ask questions to make sure your paper is good.

I understand the bad reviews, but at the same time I don't. First, Scott can easily come across as stuffy - but I think the accent does a lot of that for him. He's also into modernity... which I'm not a fan of. BUT he is smart and he really wants his students to do well. He grades a little nicer than fair. In the end bad students deserve bad grades
Worst. Professor. Ever. RUN!

Scott really wants everyone to do well in his class. If you don't show interest and demonstrate an effort to succeed don't expect him to spoon feed you. He marks fairly, again, if you put in the effort you get good grades, if you don't care to work hard than don't expect an "A". He is also pleasant to have a conversation with, very approachable.
He has high standards but he clearly put a lot of effort into his lectures. If you pay attention in the lectures you can do well in his class.

Horrible prof. This was the first prof that I ever had that came in the tutorial and swore at us just cause we didn't do the readings one time. lectures are boring to the point that not attending is basically the same as going. Highly would not recommend

His philosophy background is apparent in lectures. He has an impressive vocabulary, which is advanced for first year. The assignment structure was fair and easy. Essay questions are somewhat vague, allowing for students to think on their own. He has high expectations that students care about engaging with material, which is not unreasonable.
He can make a simple thing very complicated. Papers are extremely hard and the guidelines are very vague. So hard to stay awake in his lecture.

so hard to understand what he is saying most of the time. lectures are not helpful at all. thank god for the nice TA. take him if you don't care to learn anything and are good at writting essays with no help.
most boring lecture I have ever attended in my life. The guy still loves philosophy so he heavily incorporates it into the lecture. Tough, boring, dry, not a class I would recommend

Scott will go to great lengths to help you if you show interest in his class. Sometimes the material can be a bit confusing or go over your head but the more you talk about it the easier it is to understand.

Scott is the least favorite ta i have ever had. I recommend avoiding him if possible. He has a large ego that he uses to make you feel bad. He spent 1.5 hrs total this semester telling us how lazy we all were and how we were screwed for our futures bc we dont read 9 hrs a week per class which is what he unrealistically thinks we should be doing. He is a tough marker so make sure you work hard. Overall, arrogant, rude and obnoxious best describe scott.

For the first few lectures he read off of his notes like a presentation but after a while, when he started to speak from experience and knowledge his lectures were more interesting. His lecture content isn't really related to textbook but exams will be on both. Attend class and read your articles!
I have the biggest brain-crush on both Gary and Scott. I will be on the look out for classes they teach next Fall.

Scott's not as bad as he seems. High expectations and not shy about insulting your inevitable laziness and lack of intelligence, but if you're THAT wounded by it then your ego is too fragile anyways. Always willing to help, and though you will be too scared to ask for it, he'll likely end up telling you some interesting/useful things anyways.

Scott was an excellent ta, though he often came off as snobby and a know it all. my biggest regret in this class is that i didn't ask him to go to the pub after our final class. One of the most interesting, well spoken, rational and coherent people I have ever met. my cmns exam tmrw and am 100% confident that i am going to do great. THANK YOU SCOTT
awful!! dont ever take a tutorial with him hes the worst T.A. ive ever had

Scott is a hard marker and takes the content beyond the level of the course. He likes to go on philosophical rants that don't end up proving anything at all. I went to his classes more for entertainment value than to learn. Do not have him as your TA if you are used to good marks.

worst ta ever. if it wasn't for participation marks i wouldn't even attend and i didn't even end up with a good mark. likes to pick on students and probably favours. i get out of tutorial feeling like i wasted an hour sitting there starring into space. he is NOT helpful at all.

scott was the worst ta ive ever had. one snotty guy who is a hard and unfair marker . i dont know how im going to pass the final under the logic behind his marking. i have nothing good to say about him, and am suprised that some people actually rate him well.
Scott is rude, arrogant, and egotistical. He is uninterested in your opinion, although he asks for it in the first place.
Hot as hell. I have no idea what this class was about.

has bad breathe which is likely because he is genetically recessive. stuffy and overly philosophical for a course on everyday life. I must admit though that he really is a complete sex bomb. God help us if he becomes a prof.
Will miss his grandiosity
Is purely interested in philosophy.

Scott was by far one of the worst TA's I have ever had. Given that this class title is communication in everday life, Scott seems to overlook that and focuses more on making life and class impossible to complete. He is extremely arrogant and very difficult to approach. Personally I have never met a worse TA. Communication becomes flawed bc of him.
Good

I've had tough TAs but it was always in "tough love." This guy goes out of his way to make life difficult (see below comments.) Half my tutorial switched or dropped. If you decide to tough it out and Scot goes too far, email the professor. It was only then that Scot stopped kicking people out and the proposal was changed back to 2 pages.

Horrible person. Kicked people out the first class for being even 2 min. late. Rude, mean, unreachable expectations. Having him as a teacher is a big regret.  I hope karma makes him suffer the way we have to suffer with him. I don't know how I will be able to get through this class.  Horrible person.

The first rating is totally bogus. Scott is mean, aggressive, uses unprofessional language, lies about assignment expectations (says a proposal is 8-10 pages when it's really only 2), kicks people out of class if they show up 5 min. late, and constantly interrupts the tutorial to chastise the late student. Hard marker, readings mandatory, scary guy
No Comments

He is a great TA who helps his students and stuff. Super easy going guy, really friendly, and nice. He totally grades easy, so if your into an easy A TAKE HIS CLASS. I like, did no readings a nd got an A+. LOVE HIM!
